title: Unit 4 - A Day in the Kitchen With Grandma
subtitle: Unit 4
meanings: A piece of thick absorbent cloth or paper used for drying or wiping
A hard white object in the mouth used for biting and chewing
Long, thin strips of pasta made from flour, water, and eggs
A tall tropical plant with hard, hollow stems used as building material or food
A thick mixture of flour and liquid, used for baking into bread or pastry
Small lumps of dough cooked by steaming or boiling, often with a filling
The necessary items for a particular purpose or activity
A large electric appliance used to keep food and drinks cold
Pressed or pounded into small fragments or powder
Marked or discolored with something that is difficult to remove
Gave a particular form or outline to something
Made something look more attractive by adding extra items or images
Mixed an ingredient into a liquid using a spoon or stick
Cooked in water that has reached the temperature at which it bubbles
Cooked in hot fat or oil
sentences: She used the kitchen towel to wipe the oily table.
Grandma broke a tooth while she was eating some chilli crabs.
I ordered chicken noodles for dinner.
She wrapped the rice with bamboo leaves.
Karen and her mother kneaded the dough to make bread.
We eat rice dumplings during the Dragon Boat Festival.
My parents went shopping for new kitchen equipment for our new house.
Please keep the milk in the refrigerator.
Grandma crushed the blue flowers to colour the rice.
His shirt was stained with ink.
The chef shaped the mixture into a ball.
My siblings and I decorated the gingerbread cookies with icing.
He stirred some milk into his coffee.
Grandma boiled some eggs to add to the salad.
My mother fried fish for yesterday's dinner.
